title: Low-temperature mixed--refrigerant for hydrogen precooling in large scale
abstract: The present invention relates to a refrigerant composition. According to the invention it is envisioned that the composition comprises comprising an inert gas selected from nitrogen, argon, neon and a mixture thereof, and a mixture of at least two C 1 -C 5 hydrocarbons. The present invention further relates to the use of the refrigerant composition in a method for liquefying a gaseous substance, particularly hydrogen or helium.
claims:
Claims Claims 1 . Refrigerant composition comprising four components, wherein a first component is nitrogen or nitrogen in a mixture with neon and/or argon, a second component is methane or argon, a third component is ethane or ethylene, and a fourth component is one of n-butane, isobutane, 1 -butene, propane, propylene, n-pentane and isopentane. 2. The refrigerant composition according to claim 1 , wherein said composition further comprises a fifth component, wherein the fifth component is one of n- butane, isobutane, propane, propylene, n-pentane and isopentane, wherein said the fifth component is different from said fourth component. 3. The refrigerant composition according to one of the claims 1 to 3, wherein said composition further comprises a sixth component, wherein the sixth component is one of n-butane, isobutane, propane, propylene, n-pentane and isopentane, wherein said sixth component is different from said fourth component and said fifth component. 4. The refrigerant composition according to one of the claims 1 to 4, wherein said composition comprises - nitrogen in the range of 5 mol. % to 35 mol. %, and - methane in the range of 20 mol. % to 40 mol. %, and - ethane or ethylene in the range of 10 mol. % to 45 mol. %, and - 1 - butene, n-butane, isobutane, n-pentane or isopentane in the range of 10 mol. % to 35 mol. % or propane or propylene in the range of 10 mol. % to 45 mol. %, provided that the sum of the above concentrations does not exceed 100 mol. %. 5. The refrigerant composition according to one of the claims 1 to 4, wherein said composition comprises - nitrogen in the range of 5 mol. % to 35 mol. %, and - argon in the range of 20 mol. % to 40 mol. %, and - ethane or ethylene in the range of 10 mol. % to 40 mol. %, and 

 isobutane, isopentane or 1 -butene in the range of 10 mol. % to 35 mol. %, or propane or propylene in the range of 10 mol. % to 45 mol. %, provided that the sum of the above concentrations does not exceed 100 mol. % The refrigerant composition according to claims 1 or claim 4, wherein said composition comprises - 10 mol. % to 23 mol. % nitrogen, and - 27 mol. % to 33 mol. % methane, and - 1 1 mol. % to 38 mol. % ethane, and - 16 mol. % to 30 mol. % n-butane, isobutane, isopentane, 1 -butene or isobutane, or 20 mol. % to 40 mol. % propane, provided that the sum of the concentrations of the above-mentioned components does not exceed 100 mol %. The refrigerant composition according to any of of claims 1 4 and 6, wherein said composition consists of - 1 1 mol. % nitrogen, 33 mol. % methane, 31 mol. % ethane and 25 mol. % n- butane, or - 12 mol. % nitrogen, 32 mol. % methane, 31 mol. % ethane and 25 mol. % n- butane, or - 14 mol. % nitrogen, 32 mol. % methane, 29 mol. % ethane and 25 mol. % isobutane, or - 16 mol. % nitrogen, 31 mol. % methane, 27 mol. % ethane and 26 mol. % isobutane, or - 10 mol. % nitrogen, 33 mol. % methane, 41 mol. % ethane and 16 mol. % n- pentane, or - 1 1 mol. % nitrogen, 32 mol. % methane, 38 mol. % ethane and 19 mol. % isopentane, or - 22 mol. % nitrogen, 30 mol. % methane, 24 mol. % ethane and 24 mol. % isobutane, or - 17 mol. % nitrogen, 33 mol. % methane, 24 mol. % ethane and 26 mol. % isobutane, or - 18 mol. % nitrogen, 29 mol. % methane, 36 mol. % ethane and 17 mol. % isopentane, or 

 - 18 mol. % nitrogen, 27 mol. % methane, 37 mol. % ethane and 18 mol. % isopentane, or - 23 mol. % nitrogen, 30 mol. % methane, 12 mol. % ethane and 35 mol. % propane, or - 20 mol. % nitrogen, 30 mol. % methane, 26 mol. % ethane and 24 mol. % 1 - butene, or - 20 mol. % nitrogen, 30 mol. % methane, 24 mol % ethane and 26 mol. % 1 - butene, or - 22 mol. % nitrogen, 29 mol. % methane, 1 1 mol. % ethane and 38 mol. % propane, or - 18 mol. % nitrogen, 27 mol. % methane, 37 mol. % ethane and 18 mol. % isopentane, or - 23 mol. % nitrogen, 29 mol. % methane, 24 mol. % ethane and 24 mol. % isobutane. 8. The refrigerant according to one of the claims 1 , 2, and 5, wherein said composition consists of 22 mol. % nitrogen, 29 mol. % methane, 28 mol. % ethane, 12 mol. % isobutane and 9 mol. % isopentane 9. The refrigerant composition according to claim 1 or claim 56, wherein said composition comprises 12.5 mol. % nitrogen, 38 mol. % argon, 25.5 mol. % ethane and 24 mol. % 1 -butene. 10. Use of a refrigerant composition according to one of the claims 1 to 9, particularly as a precooling refrigerant, for liquefying a gaseous substance. 1 1 . Method for liquefying a feed gas stream, the method comprises the steps of: - providing a feed gas stream (1 1 ) comprising a gas, wherein said feed gas stream (1 1 ) has initial temperature, - precooling said feed gas stream (1 1 ) from said initial temperature to an intermediate temperature in a precooling step by a closed loop cooling cycle with a precooling refrigerant stream (41 ) yielding a precooled feed gas stream (12), 

 - cooling said precooled feed gas stream (12) in a cooling step from said intermediate temperature to a temperature below the boiling temperature or the critical temperature of said gas, wherein said precooling refrigerant stream (41 ) comprises or consists of a refrigerant composition according to one of the claims 1 to 9. 12. The method according to claim 1 1 , wherein said feed gas is selected from the group comprised of hydrogen and helium. 13. The method according to claim 1 1 or 12, wherein said precooling step comprises the steps of: - providing said precooling refrigerant stream (41 ) with a first pressure, - expanding said precooling refrigerant stream (41 ) in a first expansion device (64) to a second pressure yielding an expanded precooling refrigerant stream (42), - guiding said expanded precooling refrigerant stream and said feed gas stream (1 1 ) such that heat can indirectly be transferred between the expanded precooling refrigerant stream (42) and said feed gas stream (1 1 ), thereby particularly cooling said feed gas stream (1 1 ) to said intermediate temperature, and - compressing said expanded precooling refrigerant (42) to said first pressure in a first precooling compressor yielding said second refrigerant stream (41 ). 14. The method according to any of claims 1 1 to 13, wherein said feed gas stream (1 1 ) comprises hydrogen and is precooled in said precooling step to a temperature equal or above 80 K, particularly in the range of 85 K to 120 K, yielding said precooled feed gas stream (12), and said precooled feed gas stream (12) is brought into contact with a catalyst being able to catalyse the conversion of ortho hydrogen to para hydrogen.
